core.note: Central infrastructure — without cross-app dependencies, almost all other applications depend on it.

heslar.note: Controlled vocabularies (číselníky). Imported by almost all domain applications.

xml_generator.note: Generator of XML outputs. Contains BaseAmcrModel and ModelWithMetadata. CIRCULAR DEPENDENCY with adb.

uzivatel.note: Users, organizations, permissions, CAS SSO. Imported by many applications.

projekt.note: The main domain entity — archaeological projects. CIRCULAR DEPENDENCY with oznameni.

oznameni.note: Project notifiers. CIRCULAR DEPENDENCY with projekt (mutual imports at the models.py level).

arch_z.note: Archaeological records. A highly interconnected domain application.

dokument.note: Documents and attachments. The highest number of imported applications (8 cross-app imports).

adb.note: Archaeological database. CIRCULAR DEPENDENCY with xml_generator (models.py ↔ generator.py).

pian.note: Spatial identification of finds — geometry and coordinate transformations.

notifikace_projekty.note: Email notifications via Celery. No tests.

fedora_management.note: Fedora Commons management of digital objects.

circular_dependencies.description: projekt and oznameni import each other. oznameni/models.py imports Projekt from projekt/models.py; projekt/forms.py, views.py, and urls.py import from oznameni.
evidence: webclient/projekt/forms.py: from oznameni.forms import DateRangeField, DateRangeWidget
circular_dependencies.risk: The import oznameni.models → projekt.models is at the module level. Django usually handles this during bootstrapping thanks to lazy loading, but adding further imports or moving logic may cause an ImportError at any time.
circular_dependencies.recommendation: Merge oznameni into projekt, or extract the shared logic into a core/shared module.

circular_dependencies.description: adb/models.py imports from xml_generator/models.py (BaseAmcrModel, ModelWithMetadata); xml_generator/generator.py imports from adb/models.py (VyskovyBod at the module level, Adb lazily inside a function).
evidence: webclient/adb/models.py: from xml_generator.models import BaseAmcrModel, ModelWithMetadata
webclient/xml_generator/generator.py: from adb.models import VyskovyBod  # module-level import
webclient/xml_generator/generator.py: from adb.models import Adb  # lazy import inside a function
circular_dependencies.risk: The module-level import of VyskovyBod in generator.py may cause problems when extending xml_generator.models or changing the application load order. The lazy import of Adb partially mitigates the situation.
circular_dependencies.recommendation: Move BaseAmcrModel and ModelWithMetadata into core or a dedicated amcr_base module. Separate xml_generator from the specific domain models.

architectural_issues.description: The `core` application is imported by 16+ applications. It has 77 Python files and 26 migrations — a violation of the single-responsibility principle. Any change in core has a potentially global impact.
architectural_issues.recommendation: Decompose core into smaller, thematic modules (core.permissions, core.logging, core.middleware, core.signals).

architectural_issues.description: The `dokument` application imports 8 other applications (the highest fan-out). The high degree of dependencies indicates excessive responsibility or tight coupling.
architectural_issues.recommendation: Audit whether some dependencies can be replaced with signals or looser coupling via a services layer.

architectural_issues.description: The `xml_generator` application is imported by 10 applications and at the same time imports 3 others. It serves as cross-cutting infrastructure (BaseAmcrModel, ModelWithMetadata), but together with the generator it forms an unhealthy dependency.
architectural_issues.recommendation: Split xml_generator into `amcr_base` (base models) and `xml_generator` (only the XML generator). This removes the circular dependency CIRC-02.

architectural_issues.description: The `cron` and `notifikace_projekty` applications have no tests (see AGENTS.md). Cron imports 6 applications without a safety net of tests.
architectural_issues.recommendation: Add unit tests for the Celery tasks in cron and notifikace_projekty.

python_dependencies.note: No pyproject.toml was found. All Python dependencies are managed via requirements.txt.

concerns.note: A testing dependency is included in the production requirements.txt. It should be separated into requirements-test.txt.
The debug toolbar and django-livereload-server are development tools in the production requirements.txt — they needlessly burden the production image.
A developer tool in the production requirements.txt.
A testing dependency in the production requirements.txt.
Without a version specification — may cause non-deterministic builds.

npm_dependencies.note: No package.json was found in the repository. Frontend dependencies are probably managed via CDN. Details passed to T07.

celery_worker.note: Celery worker — processing of asynchronous tasks.

redis.note: Cache and Celery broker — a core service.

logstash.note: Log pipeline. Without an explicit depends_on on elasticsearch — a potential race condition at startup.

selenium.note: Standalone Chromium for the Selenium tests.

issues.description: logstash has no depends_on: elasticsearch, even though it reads from / writes to it. A failure may occur on concurrent startup.
issues.recommendation: Add depends_on: - elasticsearch to the logstash service definition.

tightly_coupled_modules.reason: Imported by 16+ applications. Contains middleware, permissions, constants, signals, logging — too many responsibilities.
Imported by 16 applications as the central controlled vocabulary. This coupling is natural for an architecture with controlled vocabularies.
Imported by 10 applications — contains BaseAmcrModel, which should belong in core or a shared base module.
Imported by 11 applications. Natural for an application managing users, but worth attention.
Imports 8 applications (the highest fan-out). A potential violation of the single-responsibility principle.
